meta.title: Offshore Safety Case — Argument Map (assistive structure)
meta.subtitle: Named installation — SI 2015/398 Schedule 6/7 claim → argument → evidence
meta.author: offshore-safety-case (assistive)
meta.classification: Confidential — competent-person review required; precedes competent-authority acceptance
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This is an ASSISTIVE structuring of the duty-holder's offshore safety case under SI 2015/398 (SCR 2015), laid out as a goal-structured claim → argument → evidence map. It records the duty-holder's content and the externally-produced QRA/consequence figures with their provenance; it does not produce the safety case autonomously, does not compute or invent any quantitative figure, and does not assert any barrier/SCE effective without a cited performance standard. Unsupplied elements, figures, and barrier-effectiveness claims are recorded [GAP]. This output PRECEDES competent-authority acceptance — it is not, and does not assert, an accepted safety case.

Top claim (to be demonstrated by the duty-holder's evidence): major-accident hazards on the installation are controlled so that risks to personnel and the environment are reduced to As Low As Reasonably Practicable (ALARP).

rows: Identify MAH & major-accident scenarios from the duty-holder's PHA/HAZID/HAZOP
Demonstrate prevention + detection + mitigation barriers, each with a performance standard
Record the external QRA/consequence analysis with provenance; never computed here
[GAP] — supply the external QRA and ALARP cost-benefit
List the safety-and-environmental-critical elements and the performance standard each must meet
Record the verifier and the verifier findings against the SCE performance standards
Cross-reference the marine-emergency-response (MAR-03) EER plan — not rebuilt here
sections.caption: Goal-structured safety-case argument — element / strategy / evidence / status. A row with no supplied evidence is [GAP], never asserted Supported.

items: Bowtie analyses for each major-accident hazard — [GAP] until supplied
HAZID / HAZOP study reports with closed actions — [GAP] until supplied
External QRA / consequence-modelling report with provenance — [GAP] until supplied (the skill records, never computes)
SCE register with performance standards and verifier findings — [GAP] until supplied
Emergency response (EER) — see the marine-emergency-response (MAR-03) plan

rows.title: External QRA / ALARP demonstration not supplied
rows.description: The ALARP demonstration cannot be assembled without the duty-holder's external QRA. Recorded [GAP] — no figure is invented or computed.

rows.title: SCE performance standards / verifier findings not supplied
rows.description: No SCE/barrier is asserted effective without a cited performance standard or verifier finding. Recorded [GAP].

rows.control: Inherently safer design — process inventory minimisation
Fire & gas detection with automatic deluge (SCE — performance standard [GAP] until supplied)

rows.action: Supply the external QRA / consequence analysis so the ALARP demonstration can be recorded with provenance
Supply the SCE register, performance standards and the independent verifier findings

sections.text: This is decision-support that PRECEDES competent-authority acceptance. It does not assert, and must not be read as, an accepted/approved/authorised safety case. A competent person must review it; acceptance is the act of HSE + OPRED.

items: Offshore Installations (Offshore Safety Directive) (Safety Case etc.) Regulations 2015 — SI 2015/398 (current; SCR 2005 is the superseded legacy reference)
ALARP demonstration principles (external QRA recorded, never computed here)
